most_recent_enforcement_milestone.description: Chapter III Section 4, Chapter V, Chapter VII and Chapter XII and Article 78 came into application (includes obligations for providers of general-purpose AI models and certain governance provisions).

primary_source.title: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 (Artificial Intelligence Act) - Official Journal
primary_source.citation_line: Article 113: 'Chapters I and II shall apply from 2 February 2025; Chapter III Section 4, Chapter V, Chapter VII and Chapter XII and Article 78 shall apply from 2 August 2025'
primary_source.ref_id: turn3view0 / turn5view0 (dataguidance pdf copy of OJ L, 12.7.2024)

next_obligations.near_term: 2026-08-02 - majority of rules come into application (rules for high-risk AI in Annex III, transparency rules under Article 50, enforcement by national and EU authorities, innovation support measures)
next_obligations.later: 2027-08-02 - Article 6(1) and corresponding obligations apply; additional high-risk rules for AI embedded in regulated products apply

sources.title: AI Act Service Desk - Timeline for the Implementation of the EU AI Act
sources.note: timeline page; outlines 02 Feb 2025, 02 Aug 2025, 02 Aug 2026, 02 Aug 2027 milestones

sources.title: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 - Article 113 (Entry into force and application)

key_uncertainties_for_product_teams: Classification ambiguity: how Article 6 and delegated/guidance acts will define which systems are 'high-risk' in practice (implementing/delegated acts & Commission guidance expected).

Harmonised standards & conformity assessments: availability/timing of harmonised standards, notified bodies and conformity assessment templates will affect how to demonstrate compliance (some implementing acts due before applicability dates).
GPAI systemic-risk designation: thresholds and timing (notification to AI Office; Commission may designate models presenting systemic risk).

Enforcement capacity & national divergence: Member States must adopt penalty rules and designate competent authorities by application dates — differences across countries are likely until enforcement practice stabilises.
Interplay with other laws and secondary acts: GDPR, sectoral product safety/medical/device law interactions, and upcoming implementing/delegated acts (timing uncertain).

sources_notes.source: European Commission - 'AI Act enters into force' (press release)
